title: add --mount for buidah run
body:
close #1597

Enables to use path with colon when using `buildah run --volume` to bind a volume. Have the same functionality as --volume.

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
LGTM
If you copied these functions out of libpod, can we remove them from libpod and vendor these in?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 1d11851) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
This doesn't support  type=tmpfs, where should I add the implementation for that?
@QiWang19 I guess in a separate PR.
@rhatdan I have not idea where to add the --mount implememtation into the code or how --mount should be implemented :neutral_face:, checking the code in libpod...

I think we'll need to adjust the logic to avoid adding the `rbind` option to `options` when the `mountType` doesn't end up set to "bind", in the `parseMount()` helper function in `Builder.runSetupVolumeMounts()` in `run_linux.go`.
There's a typo in the commit log.
tmpfs can work without adding rbind to the options. Is this because of the system call. Are these the options will be sent to the system call?

Some of the options are parsed are used to set or clear bits in the `mountflags` value that's passed to mount(2), and the rest are passed directly as text.  The `bind` and `rbind` options become flags which cause the filesystem type to be ignored, so they can't be specified if we want to mount a tmpfs.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 0f7f7a1) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
LGTM assuming happy tests.  I'll give @rhatdan a chance to look at it tomorrow, if he doesn't merge it, I will over tea.

:pushpin: Commit 94e2554 has been approved by `rhatdan`
